encounter.location: Triangle Pediatrics, 4201 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 200, Raleigh, NC 27607

conditions.name: severe persistent asthma
conditions.note: Improved on current step-up regimen (Advair HFA 115/21). Rescue inhaler use decreased to approximately once per week. No ED visits or hospitalizations since January 2025 exacerbation. No step-down warranted at this time.

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis
atopic dermatitis
conditions.note: Well controlled. Mild dry patches on bilateral forearms only, no active excoriation or lichenification.

conditions.name: allergic rhinitis
conditions.note: Controlled on cetirizine 5 mg daily. Mild seasonal exacerbation expected with spring pollen.

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair HFA 115/21) inhaler
medications.sig: Inhale 1 puff by mouth twice daily using spacer device

medications.name: montelukast 4 mg chewable tablet
medications.sig: Chew 1 tablet by mouth once daily at bedtime

medications.name: albuterol (ProAir HFA 90 mcg) inhaler
medications.sig: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 4-6 hours as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath, using spacer

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen Jr 0.15 mg) two-pack
medications.sig: Inject 0.15 mg intramuscularly into outer thigh as needed for signs of anaphylaxis; carry at all times
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis, tree nut allergy

medications.name: cetirizine 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take 5 mg by mouth once daily

medications.name: triamcinolone 0.1% ointment
medications.sig: Apply thin layer to affected areas twice daily as needed for flares

document_references.description: Comprehensive 5-year well-child visit note documenting vitals, developmental screening confirming kindergarten readiness, vision and hearing screening results (both passed), physical examination findings, reassessment of severe persistent asthma (improved on Advair HFA 115/21), food allergies (stable, EpiPen Jr renewed), atopic dermatitis (well controlled), allergic rhinitis (controlled), and growth/development assessment. Plan includes continuation of current asthma, allergy, and eczema regimens with follow-up in 3 months.

Updated 504 plan documentation prepared for kindergarten entry, accommodating severe persistent asthma (rescue inhaler access, activity modifications) and food allergies (peanut and tree nut avoidance, anaphylaxis action plan, EpiPen availability at school).
